content: Just as the language learning definition of reflexion frames it as a practice of reflecting on output to refine linguistic mastery, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to adopt the graded, feedback-driven structure of high school language writing reflection workshops to improve their error correction capabilities. This structured, pedagogy-grounded reflexion framework outperforms current unstructured LLM self-reflection by explicitly grading inconsistent reasoning outputs the same way teachers grade student drafts to highlight conceptual gaps.
